Weekly Podcast
            The Explainer
            The Explainer is a weekly podcast from TheJournal.ie that takes a deeper look at one big news story you need to know about. Listen now wherever you get your podcasts

The42
                    
                    
                        The latest Irish and international sports news for readers and members

Noteworthy
                    
                    
                        A platform helping fund the type of in-depth journalism that the public wants to see

More than 20 people killed in south Iran floods
‘My heart was broken by the All-Irelands we lost’ - Jim Carney’s long life in GAA
Poll: At what age would you like to retire?

Good Morning - 7 hours ago            
            The 9 at 9
            Details of Irish terror arrests last year, Ukraine-Russia grain deal and further delays at Port of Dover.
UK calls on France to act as travellers face more delays and gridlock at Port of Dover
'He'll miss it terribly' - Saluting the Sunday Game career of Pat Spillane before final curtain
Biden responding well to treatment and Covid-19 symptoms improving, doctors say

Taoiseach pays emotional visit to Singapore prison camp that held his uncle
Battle of Britain fly past over Dublin skyline to celebrate 100 years of the Irish Air Corps
Know your sport? Take our weekly quiz

Keith Ricken steps down as Cork football manager with John Cleary set to take over

Evening Wrap - 23 hours ago            
            The 5 at 5
            Five minutes, five stories, five o’clock…
Man killed and woman critical after two-car crash in Louth
‘Significant’ price hikes faced by Irish dairy, meat and grain producers
FactFind: How have other countries regulated Airbnb and what happened when they did?
Debunked: A drone image of lightning striking the Poolbeg towers has been digitally altered
FactCheck: Is the protocol stopping Jewish people in the North from practising their religion?
Poll: At what age would you like to retire?
Taoiseach Micheál Martin previously said the Government
Larry Donnelly: My first Galway GAA match changed my life - I'll be cheering them on tomorrow
Our columnist may be from Boston but he is a committed Galway fan – they take on Kerry in the All-Ireland final tomorrow.
GP: Why are we waging a war on e-cigarettes when they help smokers quit?
Dr Garrett McGovern argues that banning e-cigarette flavours will increase smoking harm and death and will not protect young people.
Opinion: In order to tackle the climate crisis, we must look to collective action
Dr Patrick Bresnihan and Dr Fiadh Tubridy write that action on social problems should be considered a core part of the climate movement.
Money Diaries: An administration assistant on €31K living in Co Clare
This week, our reader enjoys cooking at home and cycling to keep fit while saving for a long-term plan of investing in a property.
Opinion: Is the Ukraine invasion finally whipping back the curtain to reveal the backstage grime of society?
Author Sam O’Brien explores what the current geopolitical situation tells us about what lies behind echelons of power.
